authored_register.distribution_impact: Permissive; safe to vendor or copy verbatim into our self-hosted multi-tenant SaaS + on-prem + air-gap distribution with attribution. No copyleft on our code. Network-use unrestricted.
obligations: preserve LICENSE + NOTICE files for any copied code
attribution in NOTICE
no warranty disclaimer retained

authored_register.source_system: asrcm / RAPTOR / vista-dashboard-rules / aware / preproc-checklist / avs / maternity-tracker / ehmp-app / pophealth / health-data-standards
authored_register.distribution_impact: Permissive; safe to vendor, port-adapt, or run as background service in all CuraOS deployment models. health-data-standards/pophealth Ruby safe to run behind a service boundary with NOTICE. ehmp-app MIT copyable with attribution.
obligations: attribution + NOTICE for copied code (Apache-2.0)
retain MIT copyright header where MIT code is copied
license file for any verbatim copy or vendored Ruby lib

authored_register.distribution_impact: File-level (weak) copyleft: any MPL FILE we copy or modify must stay MPL and have source disclosed; mixing into our proprietary service is allowed only at file boundaries. To keep our codebase unencumbered, do NOT copy MPL files; port the PATTERN/data-model as fresh original code instead.
obligations: if any MPL file is copied: keep it MPL, disclose source of that file, retain headers
preferred: pattern-reference-only (port concept, write fresh) to avoid file-level obligations
legal-review before copying any MPL file

authored_register.distribution_impact: Strong network-use copyleft: AGPL requires that users interacting with the software OVER A NETWORK receive the complete corresponding source. For a self-hosted multi-tenant SaaS this would force open-sourcing the entire interacting service. Copying or linking AGPL code is INCOMPATIBLE with our commercial/proprietary distribution. Data-model SHAPES (facts) are not copyrightable and may be referenced.
obligations: NEVER copy or link AGPL code into any CuraOS service
data-model field-set shapes may be mined as facts and re-implemented fresh
legal-review-required before any use beyond fact-level reference
document the clean-room boundary in PR

authored_register.distribution_impact: Strong copyleft: copying or statically/dynamically linking GPL code forces the combined work under GPL. Incompatible with our proprietary multi-tenant SaaS + on-prem distribution. Standards-defined structures (X12 5010 ANSI segments, CMS-1500/UB-04 government form layouts, FHIR resources) are NOT copyrightable and may be re-implemented. Port LOGIC/data-model as fresh original code only.
obligations: NEVER copy GPL source (PHP/Java) verbatim into our codebase
X12/HCFA/UB-04 are ANSI/government standards - segment+box structure reusable, but implement fresh
port business logic + data model as original code (clean-room)
legal-review-required for any structure lifted from GPL source
document standards-vs-source provenance in PR

authored_register.source_system: odoo (community addons: account, sale, purchase, project, hr_*, stock, crm, payment)
authored_register.distribution_impact: Lesser GPL: linking against unmodified LGPL libraries is allowed without copylefting our code, BUT modifications to LGPL files must be released under LGPL, and the LGPL component must remain replaceable. We do NOT consume Odoo as a runtime library (it is Python/monolith); we port its data models + business logic as fresh TS, which avoids LGPL obligations entirely. Odoo Enterprise modules are proprietary (excluded).
obligations: do NOT copy LGPL Python files verbatim (would impose LGPL on those files)
port data models + state machines + logic as original TypeScript (clean-room)
never reference Odoo Enterprise (proprietary) modules
legal-review if any LGPL file is copied rather than re-implemented

authored_register.distribution_impact: frappe (MIT) is permissive: safe to copy with attribution. erpnext (GPL-3.0) is strong copyleft: same constraint as OpenEMR - port logic/data-model as fresh code, do not copy GPL source. The two are split: prefer MIT frappe primitives (AutoRepeat, AssignmentRule, Workflow, report.py, DocType, field-perms, webhook) for any copy; treat erpnext business modules (pricing_rule, subscription, sales/purchase/asset/fiscal) as GPL reference-only port-adapt.
obligations: frappe MIT: retain copyright header + attribution where copied
erpnext GPL: NEVER copy verbatim; port logic+model as original code
legal-review for any erpnext structure lifted
keep the frappe-MIT vs erpnext-GPL provenance explicit per copied unit

authored_register.distribution_impact: Strong copyleft, same as OpenEMR/erpnext. Facture/contrat/online-signature PHP cannot be copied. Port invoice/contract data-model + state-machine as fresh TS; adopt the signed_status enum + public-sign UX as design patterns (UX is not copyrightable as expression of an interaction concept, but implement fresh).
obligations: NEVER copy GPL PHP verbatim
port data model + state machine as original code
legal-review for any structure lifted
document clean-room boundary

authored_register.distribution_impact: GPL/AGPL: entityDef field sets and hooks cannot be copied. Mine field-set shapes (Lead/Opportunity/Account/Contact/Case/InboundEmail, probability hook, EntityManager) as data-model facts; re-implement fresh. Any AGPL-flagged module is network-use copyleft = strictly reference-only.
obligations: NEVER copy GPL/AGPL code
data-model field sets as facts re-implemented fresh
legal-review-required, especially for AGPL modules
document fact-level vs code boundary

authored_register.distribution_impact: Both permissive cores: safe to vendor/port the OSS core (Apache-2.0 / MIT) with attribution. Avoid Activepieces Enterprise Edition (EE) modules which carry a commercial license. Use the OSS connector framework (createPiece), trigger, action, agent/MCP, and registry patterns freely.
obligations: attribution + NOTICE (Apache-2.0 node-red)
retain MIT header (activepieces core)
exclude Activepieces EE (commercial) modules - verify per-file license
legal-review if any EE-licensed file is touched

authored_register.distribution_impact: Strong network-use copyleft + EE modules. Copying or linking windmill code into our networked SaaS would force AGPL source disclosure of the interacting service. Use ONLY as a design reference for scheduling/approval/concurrency/secret-backend/fairness patterns; implement fresh.
obligations: NEVER copy or link windmill code
pattern-reference only (concepts, fresh implementation)
legal-review-required
exclude any EE-licensed component entirely

authored_register.license: Sustainable Use License (source-available, fair-code; NOT OSI-approved)
authored_register.distribution_impact: NOT open-source: the Sustainable Use License restricts commercial/hosted use and embedding; copying n8n code into a commercial multi-tenant SaaS conflicts with the license. Cannot vendor, import, or copy. Usable only as a design reference for expression/pin-data/error-trigger/form-trigger SHAPES, implemented fresh.
obligations: NEVER copy n8n code or embed n8n
design-reference only (fresh implementation of concepts)
legal-review-required before any use
verify no Sustainable-Use-Licensed file is touched

authored_register.distribution_impact: License unknown = unknown risk. No code may be used until license is established. Only the data-model concept (which maps to the open FHIR FamilyMemberHistory resource - itself unencumbered) may be referenced, and even that pending confirmation.
obligations: NO code use of any kind until license confirmed
implement fresh against open FHIR FamilyMemberHistory (which carries no FamilyHistoryCPRS obligation)
legal-review-required before any reference beyond the open-FHIR mapping
flag as critical-blocker if reuse is attempted

authored_register.distribution_impact: Permissive; safe to vendor/import as a runtime dependency in commerce-core across all deployment models. Already embedded. No copyleft on our code.
obligations: retain MIT license + attribution
standard MIT - no further obligation

authored_register.source_system: local first-party services (esign-core, terminology-service, audit-core, all curaos/* code)
authored_register.distribution_impact: Our own code; no external license risk. These services already EXCEED their source equivalents (esign eIDAS/UETA, terminology Snowstorm, audit hash-chain) and are reject-inbound - no external code enters.
obligations: none external
ensure any harvested UX pattern (e.g. Dolibarr signed_status enum) is implemented fresh, not copied
